repo_notes.author: Maintainers
repo_notes.content: NEUROTOXIC: GRIND THE VOID v3.0 is a frontend-only SPA built with React 19.2.5 + Vite 8.0.8. Runtime state is managed in src/context and persisted in localStorage; no backend API is required for core gameplay.

repo_notes.author: Maintainers
repo_notes.content: Testing/tooling baseline is pnpm-first. Canonical CI workflows are .github/workflows/test.yml, deploy.yml, and lint-fix-preview.yml. Local quality gate before PRs: `pnpm run test:all`.

repo_notes.author: Architecture
repo_notes.content: Travel flow is split into two explicit paths: useTravelLogic orchestrates overworld travel selection and minigame start; useArrivalLogic orchestrates post-minigame arrival sequencing. Shared node-type behavior and travel-event triggering live in src/utils/arrivalUtils.js.

repo_notes.author: Architecture
repo_notes.content: Scene lazy loading now follows default-export convention directly in SceneRouter. GameState also exposes phased split hooks (`useGameSelector`, `useGameActions`) while legacy `useGameState` remains for compatibility.

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Entry points: src/main.jsx bootstraps, src/App.jsx routes scenes, src/context/GameState.jsx provides state/actions, src/hooks/ contains orchestration hooks, src/utils/ contains pure/domain utilities and side-effect services (audio).

pages.title: Tooling & CI
pages.purpose: Use pnpm for install and scripts. CI runs node:test suites, vitest suites, locale checks, and deployment through pnpm commands. See docs/TEST_FAST_FEEDBACK_CHANGES.md for current fast-feedback split.

pages.title: Travel & Arrival Canonical Paths
pages.purpose: Canonical orchestration: Overworld uses useTravelLogic (two-click travel confirm, resource checks, minigame dispatch/fallback). TourbusScene completion uses useArrivalLogic, which advances day/saves and delegates node-specific routing to arrivalUtils.handleNodeArrival().
